intro: Baking combines science (chemistry and physics) with tradition. When planning a birthday party, getting the cake size right keeps guests happy. A standard slice weighs about 50g, but portion sizes vary by cake type (light sponge vs. rich chocolate). Learn how many servings each cake diameter provides: 20cm → 10–12 servings, 25cm → 15–18, 30cm → 25–30. This calculator is free, fast, and helps you avoid over- or under-ordering.
keyTakeaway: **Birthday Cake Servings Guide**: 20cm cake serves 10–12 people, 25cm serves 15–18, 30cm serves 25–30. Match your guest count to the right cake size with this free calculator.
useCases: Planning the right cake size for your birthday party
explanation:
Planning a birthday party means getting the details right—including dessert. Cake sizes are typically measured by diameter, and portion counts depend on slice thickness and cake density.

## How We Calculate

Our calculator uses standard bakery portion sizes:
- **20cm cake**: 10–12 slices
- **25cm cake**: 15–18 slices
- **30cm cake**: 25–30 slices

These are based on standard slice weight (~50g per slice) and typical cake recipes. Adjust up if your guests prefer smaller slices, or down if they prefer generous portions.

## Portion Factors

Different cake types and serving styles affect portions:
- **Light sponge cakes** can be sliced thinner → more servings
- **Dense chocolate cakes** are richer → fewer servings needed
- **Sheet cakes** (rectangular) yield more slices than round cakes of the same weight
- **Formal events** often call for smaller slices; **casual parties** allow larger ones

## Important Notes

This calculator provides general guidance based on standard portion sizes. Actual servings may vary depending on cake recipe, density, and your guests' appetites. For special dietary needs or unusual requests, consult a professional baker.

faq.q: How many people does a 20cm cake serve?
faq.a: A 20cm (8-inch) round cake typically serves 10–12 people with standard slices.

faq.q: How many servings does a 25cm cake provide?
faq.a: A 25cm (10-inch) cake serves approximately 15–18 people at standard slice size.

faq.q: What size cake do I need for 25 guests?
faq.a: For 25 guests, a 30cm (12-inch) cake is ideal. For a more generous event with larger slices, consider two 25cm cakes.

faq.q: Why do different cakes have different serving counts?
faq.a: Cake density affects servings. Lighter sponge cakes can be sliced thinner, while dense chocolate cakes yield fewer slices. The recipe and type matter as much as size.

faq.q: Can I use this for sheet cakes or specialty shapes?
faq.a: This calculator is optimized for round cakes. Sheet cakes and specialty shapes often yield more servings due to their shape and typically lighter texture.
Our estimates are based on standard bakery practices and portion sizes. Actual results may vary ±10% depending on recipe, density, and slice preference.

faq.q: What if I want larger or smaller slices?
faq.a: Standard slices are about 50g each. Larger slices mean fewer servings; smaller slices mean more. Adjust based on your guests' appetites.
